text:
Australia's Olympic team has unveiled the designs they will wear during the Paris 2024 opening ceremony, with the design including a nod to past champions and Indigenous artwork.

Two unique design elements have been included in the uniforms Australians will wear at the opening ceremony of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.

The lining of the green blazer the team will be wear on the night contains the names of all 301 Australians who have been crowned summer Olympic champions.

The Australian Olympians' Oath has also been inscribed on the inside jacket pocket of the blazer, which has been created by Sportscraft and will be worn by close to 500 athletes.

The uniforms were unveiled on Wednesday, 100 days out from the opening ceremony which is set to be held outside a stadium for the first time in summer Olympic history.

"I love that the Olympians' Oath will sit on the inside lining at heart level, with the support of all Australia's Olympic champions throughout history wrapping around each of our Team members for Paris," Australia's chef de mission Anna Meares said.

"I know Aussie athletes are working hard to earn their own opportunity to don this uniform in Paris and with 100 days to go, this will add excitement and inspiration to the training and competition grind knowing this awaits their efforts."

Indigenous artwork is also a key feature of the uniform, including 'Ngalmun Danalaig (Our Way of Life)' from David Bosun, a Torres Strait Island artist, and 'Walking Together' from Olympic boxer Paul Fleming.

Those artworks have been used for the scarf, pocket square and with the oath.

Fleming's artwork is further included on the insoles of the shoes that will be worn by the athletes.

Along with the blazer, female athletes have a choice of a sport inspired tee or a tank, and either "a stone coloured chino short or a green and gold ombre pleated skirt".

Male athletes can opt for "a sports inspired tee of their own, with the stretch chino short".

The uniform - which will be on show as the opening ceremony takes place along the Seine river on July 26 - is said to be "inspired by the unmistakable essence of Paris".

"Wearing an Australian Olympic Opening Ceremony uniform is a rare and special moment," Meares, a four-time Olympian, said.

"For Olympians, it brings us together, from different sports, backgrounds and experiences, proudly uniting us through that team uniform.

"It creates a connection and sense of belonging to the Australian Olympic Team's proud history.

"It begins our Olympic experience and post-games continues to be worn by Olympians proudly to events, long past our days as elite athletes."

The uniforms Australian athletes will wear while competing in the gameswere unveiled last month.

title: Australia's Paris 2024 opening ceremony uniform revealed, with design including nod to past champions and Olympians' oath | Sky News Australia
